member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.DeadLetter.DeadLetterQueue
member.summary: Thread-safe in-memory implementation of . Stores failed data points, tracks retry attempts, and enforces a configurable capacity limit so the queue does not grow without bound.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.DeadLetter.DeadLetterQueue.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: Maximum number of entries the queue will hold at one time. Once full, the oldest resolved/permanent-failure entries are evicted first; if none are eligible, new entries are rejected and the caller is notified. Defaults to / 10.
Default retry budget applied to each entry unless overridden.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.DeadLetter.DeadLetterQueue.EvictIfNeeded
member.summary: Evicts resolved and permanently-failed entries when the queue is at capacity. If still at capacity after eviction, the oldest pending entry is removed to make room.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Domain.Models.BackpressureContext
member.summary: Manages backpressure state and flow control for the pipeline. Prevents buffer overflow and ensures graceful degradation under load.

member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this backpressure context.

member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the pipeline stage this context applies to.

member.summary: Gets or sets the current number of items in the buffer.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum buffer capacity.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether backpressure is currently active.

member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when backpressure was activated in milliseconds.

member.summary: Gets or sets the total time spent in backpressure state in milliseconds.

member.name: P:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Domain.Models.BackpressureContext.DroppedItemCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of items dropped because the buffer was at capacity. A non-zero value indicates silent data loss has occurred; callers should monitor this counter and react (e.g. raise an alert, slow down producers).

member.summary: Gets or sets the number of currently active consumers processing items.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of concurrent consumers allowed.

member.summary: Gets or sets the creation timestamp of this context.

member.summary: Gets or sets the last update timestamp of this context.

member.name: P:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Domain.Models.BackpressureContext.BackpressureEventTimestamps
member.summary: Gets or sets the queue of backpressure event timestamps for frequency calculation.

member.summary: Gets or sets custom buffer metrics as key-value pairs.

member.summary: Gets the current buffer fill percentage (0-100).

member.summary: Determines if backpressure should be applied based on buffer state.

member.summary: Attempts to add items to the buffer and triggers backpressure if needed. When the buffer is full, is incremented so callers can detect and measure data loss instead of it happening silently.

member.summary: Removes items from the buffer and may deactivate backpressure.

member.summary: Activates backpressure and records the event timestamp.

member.summary: Deactivates backpressure and accumulates backpressure duration.

member.summary: Registers a consumer and checks if max concurrency is reached.

member.summary: Unregisters a consumer and updates metrics.

member.summary: Records a custom buffer metric.

member.summary: Gets backpressure event frequency (events per minute).

member.summary: Gets a health status string for this backpressure context.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Events.PipelineEventPublisherValidation
member.summary: Provides validation helpers for instances.
member.remarks: The class is stateless with respect to validation criteria. All validation methods validate the input parameters rather than the publisher's internal state, as the publisher itself has no mutable state that requires validation.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Events.PipelineEventPublisherValidation.Validate(DotNetRealtimePipeline.Events.PipelineEventPublisher)
member.summary: Validates a instance.
member.returns: A read-only list of human-readable validation problems. Returns an empty list if the instance is valid (always the case for current implementation).
member.remarks: This method currently returns an empty list because has no validation criteria on its internal state. All members are methods that perform actions or return counts rather than maintaining mutable state.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Events.PipelineEventPublisherValidation.IsValid(DotNetRealtimePipeline.Events.PipelineEventPublisher)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified instance is valid.
member.returns: if the instance has no validation problems; otherwise, .

member.summary: Ensures that the specified instance is valid.
member.exception: Thrown when the instance is invalid; the message contains a list of validation problems.
member.remarks: This method validates the publisher instance and throws if any validation problems are found. Currently, no problems are ever found as has no validation criteria.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Integration.IRawPipelineAccess
member.summary: Opt-in interface for pipeline components that can expose their underlying primitives directly, enabling zero-copy integration with Socket, NetworkStream, ASP.NET Core request bodies, and other PipeReader/PipeWriter-aware APIs.
member.remarks: Obtaining a or via this interface bypasses the higher-level IDataStream<T> abstraction and avoids the intermediate buffer copy it introduces. Use this only when the extra throughput is worth the loss of the higher-level back-pressure and windowing guarantees — see docs/backpressure.md for guidance.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Integration.IRawPipelineAccess.AsPipeReader
member.summary: Returns the underlying for the pipeline's read side. Callers can pipe this directly into Socket.ReceiveAsync or NetworkStream.CopyToAsync without an intermediate copy.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Integration.IRawPipelineAccess.AsPipeWriter
member.summary: Returns the underlying for the pipeline's write side. Callers can write raw bytes (e.g. from a network buffer) directly into the pipeline without an intermediate copy.

member.name: F:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Metrics.BackpressureSeverity.Critical

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Metrics.BackpressureMetricsCollector
member.summary: Collects time-series backpressure metrics by polling . Records activation/release events, peak buffer levels, dropped item counts, and cumulative duration so operators and dashboards can understand back-pressure behaviour over time.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Metrics.BackpressureMetricsCollector.Poll
member.summary: Polls the current state of all registered stages and records any transitions (backpressure activated or released). Call this on a regular timer to build a meaningful history.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Metrics.BackpressureMetricsCollector.RecordManualEvent(System.String,System.Double,System.Int64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Records a manual backpressure event for a stage without waiting for a poll cycle. Useful when the caller has observed a state transition externally.

member.summary: Returns a full pipeline-wide snapshot of backpressure metrics.

member.summary: Returns all recorded events for a specific stage.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.DynamicScalingService
member.summary: Dynamically adjusts the maximum concurrent consumers of each active pipeline stage by observing per-stage backpressure metrics. Scale-up is triggered when buffer fill exceeds a configurable high-water mark; scale-down when fill stays below a low-water mark with negligible backpressure frequency. A per-stage cooldown prevents oscillation.
Initialises the service with backpressure source, pipeline configuration, and tuning parameters.
member.param: Shared service for reading and adjusting stage contexts.
Pipeline configuration providing the list of stages to monitor.
Logger for scaling decisions and diagnostic output.
Lower bound on concurrent consumers; never scales below this value.
Upper bound on concurrent consumers; never scales above this value.
Buffer fill % that triggers a scale-up (default 75).
Buffer fill % below which a scale-down is considered (default 30).
Minimum seconds between consecutive scaling actions per stage (default 15).

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.DynamicScalingService.EvaluateScalingAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Evaluates all enabled pipeline stages and applies any warranted scaling decisions. Safe to call repeatedly from a polling loop; stages still in cooldown are skipped.
member.param: Token used to cooperatively abort the evaluation pass.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.DynamicScalingService.GetScalingState(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of the current scaling state for the specified stage, or null if the stage has not yet been evaluated.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.DynamicScalingService.GetAllScalingStates
member.summary: Returns a read-only snapshot of the scaling state for all evaluated stages.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator
member.summary: Implements sliding-window aggregation over a stream of values. A new aggregate is emitted every covering the last of data, producing overlapping windows — suitable for rolling averages, anomaly detection, and other continuous analysis patterns.

member.name: P:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator.WindowSizeMs
member.summary: Size of each window in milliseconds.

member.name: P:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator.StepIntervalMs
member.summary: Interval between successive window emissions in milliseconds.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.param: Duration of each sliding window (must be > 0).
How often a new window is emitted. Must be > 0 and <= . When equal to the behaviour matches a tumbling window.

member.summary: Adds a data point to the internal buffer.

member.summary: Adds a batch of data points to the internal buffer.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator.FlushDueWindows(System.Int64)
member.summary: Emits all windows whose step boundary has been reached but not yet emitted, given that is the current wall-clock time. Expired data points (older than the oldest possible window start) are pruned from the buffer automatically.
member.param: Current time as a Unix millisecond timestamp (e.g. DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds()).
member.returns: One per step boundary crossed.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Services.SlidingWindowAggregator.FlushDueWindows
member.summary: Overload that uses the current UTC clock as currentTimeMs.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Visualization.PipelineVisualizationNode
member.summary: Represents a single stage node in the pipeline visualization graph. Carries display-time state such as buffer fill, health, and throughput.

member.summary: Stage type label (SOURCE, TRANSFORM, SINK, …).

member.summary: Current buffer fill percentage (0–100).

member.summary: Whether backpressure is currently active for this stage.

member.summary: Throughput in events per second at this stage.

member.summary: Number of items dropped at this stage.

member.name: T:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Visualization.PipelineVisualizer
member.summary: Generates human-readable ASCII diagrams of the pipeline topology. Combines static configuration with live runtime state (buffer levels, throughput, backpressure) so operators can understand pipeline health at a glance.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Visualization.PipelineVisualizer.BuildNodes(DotNetRealtimePipeline.Domain.Models.PipelineConfig)

member.summary: Renders an ASCII block diagram of the pipeline and returns it as a string. Each stage is shown as a box with live buffer, throughput, and health data. Downstream arrows connect stages top-to-bottom.

member.name: M:DotNetRealtimePipeline.Visualization.PipelineVisualizer.RenderCompact(DotNetRealtimePipeline.Domain.Models.PipelineConfig)
member.summary: Renders a compact single-line summary of the pipeline stages. Useful for embedding in log lines or status responses.

member.summary: Finds all stages in the pipeline that have critical health status. Returns stages where health is CRITICAL (backpressure active or buffer >= 95%).

member.summary: Gets a summary of throughput across all pipeline stages. Returns minimum, maximum, and average throughput in events per second.
member.returns: Tuple containing min, max, and average throughput across all stages.
